subject: fix(mobile): raise header z-index when overflow menu is open
body:
The 3-dot overflow menu was rendering behind BottomSheet (z-40) and
MobileNav (z-50) because the header's `relative` positioning created a
stacking context that capped the menu's z-50. Conditionally apply z-[60]
to the header when the menu is open so it layers above all fixed elements.

Tags: ui, mobile, z-index
Complexity: 1
Urgency: 3
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: neutral
Epic: mobile-ux
Semver: patch
